The '''hesternal tense''' in Aelaki refers specifically to events that occurred yesterday. It combines the prefix '''goki-''' with the evidential suffixes of the [[Past|past tense]].

== Formation ==

The hesternal is formed by attaching the prefix '''goki-''' to the verb stem. The same prefix is used for the [[Crastinal|crastinal]] (tomorrow), but the two are distinguished by their evidential suffixes: the hesternal uses past evidentials, while the crastinal uses future evidentials.

== Evidential suffixes ==

{| class="wikitable"
! Evidential !! Suffix !! Meaning
|-
| Auditory/Hearsay || '''-ro''' || The speaker heard about yesterday's event or learned of it through oral report
|-
| Visual/Witnessed || '''-shë''' || The speaker personally saw or witnessed yesterday's event
|-
| Inferential || '''-shëm''' || The speaker infers yesterday's event from indirect evidence
|}

These are the same suffixes used in the general [[Past|past tense]], reflecting the hesternal's nature as a more specific past frame.

== See also ==

* [[Crastinal]] -- "tomorrow" (shares the '''goki-''' prefix)
* [[Past]]
* [[Present]]
* [[Future]]
* [[Hodiernal]]
* [[Mythic]]

[[Category:grammatical tenses]]
[[Category:git synced pages]]
